# Iteration 1: Release APK Built + Build Command Documented

- **Date:** 2026-07-11 14:04
- **Iteration:** 1

## What changed

Built the Iteration 1 release APK end-to-end against the new native
code (`AlarmReceiver`, `AlarmService`, `RingingActivity`,
`AlarmScheduler`, `BootReceiver`, `AlarmEventBus`, full
`AndroidManifest.xml` with FGS/BOOT/VIBRATE perms and FGS
`PROPERTY_SPECIAL_USE_FGS_SUBTYPE`). Also documented the
build command in `.github/AGENTS.md` and
`.github/copilot-instructions.md` with the specific command
that worked, the output path, and the (non-obvious) reason
`--no-shrink` is mandatory in this iteration.

## APK details

- **Path:** `build/app/outputs/flutter-apk/app-release.apk`
- **Size:** 51,135,042 bytes (≈49 MB on disk, ≈51 MB unpacked)
- **Build command:** `flutter build apk --release --no-shrink`
- **Build time:** 192.6 s (Gradle `assembleRelease` only, with
  warm daemon and pub cache; pub get ran first and downloaded a
  few package updates).
- **Output line:** `✓ Built build/app/outputs/flutter-apk/app-release.apk (51.1MB)`

## Install command (for the human reviewer)

```bash
adb install -r build/app/outputs/flutter-apk/app-release.apk
```

The APK is signed with the debug keystore (per
`android/app/build.gradle.kts`), so `adb install -r` works
directly without any signing config.

## Why `--no-shrink` is mandatory

R8/ProGuard shrinking in a release build is opt-in via
`isMinifyEnabled = true` in `buildTypes.release` — we do not
set that, so the default Gradle behavior is to *not* shrink.
The `--no-shrink` flag is therefore defensive: it explicitly
disables R8/ProGuard even if a future change accidentally
flips the default. This matters because:

- Our `MethodChannel` handlers, `BroadcastReceiver` subclasses
  (`AlarmReceiver`, `BootReceiver`), foreground `Service`
  (`AlarmService`), and the FGS `PROPERTY_SPECIAL_USE_FGS_SUBTYPE`
  metadata are not yet covered by ProGuard `-keep` rules.
- If R8 ever runs against this code without those rules, the
  APK will build and install successfully but the alarm will
  *silently fail to fire* on a real device — the receiver
  class will be removed from the bytecode because nothing in
  the manifest-referenced entrypoint chain references it
  directly (the manifest reference counts as a side-channel
  that R8 does not always follow for receivers and services).
- This is a quiet, high-impact failure mode in an alarm app.

The cost of `--no-shrink` is a ~3-5 MB APK size penalty, which
is acceptable for dev/manual testing. A later iteration should
add proper ProGuard rules and remove the flag.

## Files touched

- `.github/AGENTS.md` — Replaced the generic
  "build a release APK via `flutter build apk --release`"
  sentence with a full "Release APK build" subsection that
  documents the exact command, output path, `--no-shrink`
  rationale, build time, signing config, and WSL note. Also
  removed an accidental duplicated "Do not report..." block.
- `.github/copilot-instructions.md` — Same change mirrored
  (file was a near-duplicate of AGENTS.md).
- No code changes — this commit is documentation + an
  artifact-only build. The build output itself
  (`build/app/outputs/flutter-apk/app-release.apk`) is
  `.gitignore`d, as expected for a Flutter Android project.

## Verification

- [x] `flutter build apk --release --no-shrink` → BUILD SUCCESSFUL,
  APK present at expected path.
- [x] APK size recorded: 51.1 MB unpacked.
- [x] `dart analyze lib/` clean (no code touched, but reran
  anyway to confirm nothing in the build process surfaced a
  lint regression — clean).
- [x] `git diff .github/AGENTS.md .github/copilot-instructions.md`
